content.full_text:
MEMBERS OF-THE CITY COUNCIL
KENNE'H E FFIESS
JERRY HARRIS
GARY L HAUSDORFER
GIL JONES
JEFF VASQUEZ
STEPHEN E JULIAN
July 10, 1992
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 "J" S treet
Sacramento, CA 95E 14
Re:
Request for Formal Opinion - San Juan Capistrano
Gentlemen:
At the direction of the City Council of San Juan Capistrano, I am requesting a
formal written opinion on the subject oI a potential conflict of interest for two
members of the City Council, At issue before the Council is a series of decisions
involving the Solag Disposal Company and operations centered at the companyrs
transler station in San Juan Capistrano. The potential lor a conllict of interest
question arises because the transler station is located within 300 feet of the real
property of the Mayor (a Councilman) and within 2500 leet of the real property of
another Councilman.
We enclose relevant materials, descriptions, documents, and a private lega.l opinion
in order to obtain as conclusive an FPPC opinion as possible. Any other material you
may require, including independent real estate appraisals of the prop€rties, will be
provided if requested. The issue has been tabled without a vote pending direction
from the FPPC or its letal staff. This is a significant matter that may shape the
future of the City of San Juan Capistrano for many years, and related issues are sure
to appear on City Council agendas lor several years to come. It is the desire of the
Council to receive guidance that may be relied upon as conclusive on the issue
whenever it arises.
NARRATIVE
t_
On July 7, 1992, the City Council oI San Juan CaPistrano took uP an item on its
regular agenda to enter into an agreement to exPand its exclusive solid waste
Iranchise agreement with Solat Disposal Company to include residential curbside
recyclint for the next seven years, including automatic renewal thereafter.
324OO PASEO ADELANTO, SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO, CALIFORNIA 92675 ' (714) 493'1171


Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Two
The City Attorney advised the Council that two ol its members should consider
abstaining on the item and withdraw from the disctssion. This was done out of an
abundance of caution based upon the physical proximity oI the Solag transfer station
in San Juan Capistrano to the business/residences of the two Council members.
One member oI the Council, Mayor Jones, withdrew and left the meeting room. The
other Councilman, Jeff Vasquez, asked for a continuance of the item to allow for an
opinion from the FPPC. He expressed a strong desire to vote on the item and
questioned whether the Solag issue could result in an impact of Sl0,000 or more on
his business or property values. There was some discussion of the possibility of
retainint an appraiser to evaluate the potential value impact on the real estate, but
no decision was made.
The matter was postponed pending this request, and the City Attorney was directed
to seek a lormal opinion from the FPPC.
ISSUES PRESENTED:
A. 
DOES MAYOR GIL JONES HAVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST THAT
WOULD PRECLUDE HIM FROM PARTICIPATING IN DECISIONS
INVOLVING CITY CONTRACTS VITH, OR THE RELOCATION OF,
THE SOLAG DISPOSAL COMPANY TRANSFER SITE IN SAN ]UAN
CAPISTRANO?
B. 
DOES COUNCILMAN JEFF VASQUEZ HAVE A CONFLICT OF
INTEREST THAT WOULD PRECLUDE HIM FROM PARTICIPATING
IN DECISIONS INVOLVING CITY CONTRACTS WITH, OR THE
RELOCATION OF, THE SOLAC DISPOSAL COMPANY TRANSFER
SITE IN SAN ]UAN CAPISTRANO?
FACTS PROVIDED:
l. 
The Jones property at )1791 Los Rios Street, San Juan Capistrano, is both a
residence and a business. The residence is an historical structure and miniature
animal farm; the business consists oI a Petting zoo of farm animals and burro
rides, with services that include on-site Parties and events, and tourist
services. The property is located approximately 250 feet from the nearest
boundary of the Solag site.
2. 
The Vasquez property at 31891 Los Rios Street, San Juan Capistrano' is both a
business and a residence. The business is a photography studio. The property is
located approximately 850 leet from the nearest boundary line of the Solag
site.
3. A diagram prepared to scale by City enSineering personnel. is Provided to
illustrite the reiative locations oI the ProPeities. (EXHIBIT A.)


Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Three
4.
The decision before the City Council on July 7, 1992, involved the approval of a
lengthy contract extending solid waste and recycling services under exclusive
franchise with Solag Disposal Company. A copy of the Agreement is
enclosed. (EXHIBIT B.)
A matter for an agenda in the near future will involve the relocation of the
Solag facility to another location in a different part oI the City. Once again,
the same properties will be involved, as well as the same City Council
members. The issue will involve the removal of the Solag transfer station from
the existint site, which will then be used for park purposes.
The Solag transfer station site is described in an enclosure to this transmittal,
which also includes an early appraisal of the Solag site for relocation
purposes. (EXHIBIT C.)
A copy of the memorandum from the City Attorney is enclosed. (EXHIBIT D.)
Due to the frequency with which conflict of interest allegations have become
the tool ol community groups opposed to Council policies, the City Attorney
has adopted the practice of scanninS the aSenda of City Council meetings for
action items involving projects, contracts or properties within 2500 feet of real
estate appearing on disclosure statements filed by City Council members. A
memorandum is prepared lor each meeting containinS this inlormation to alert
Council members of possible conflict issues. No legal conclusions are drawn
and participation is left to the discretion of each Council member.
In January of 1992, a legal opinion on related issues was prepared by the law
firm of Stradling, Yocca, Carlson & Rauth for the city and its Redevelopment
Agency. This firm is contract counsel for the Redevelopment Agency. A copy
of that opinion is provided with this transmittal. (The opinion also covers an
unrelated issue allecting the same properties of the same Council members.)
(EXHIBIT E.)
A copy of the minutes of the July 7, 1992 meeting is also enclosed. (EXHIBIT
F.)
It should also be noted that on subsequent engineering review of ProPerty maPs
for the area, two additional property interests of two additiona.l Councilmen
were identilied as lying within the 2500 foot distance from the Solag site.
a) Councilman Ken Friess disclosed "Condo Equity" in property at )1476
Calle La Purisima, San Juan CaPistranor of l0% or Sreater, with rental
income ol 'Sl,00l - S1O,0OO . This property is aPProximately 850 feet
Irom the nearest boundary of the Solag site' however, it is separated
geographically in a neighborhood containint no connectin8 roadways.
6.
7,
8.
9.


Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Four
b) Councilman Jerry Harris dsclosed a rtleasehold' interest in real property
located at 31441 Ave. de la Vista, San Juan Capistrano, of a value over
SIO0,0OO, providing income over $10,000 as a-business of "Equestrian
Services'!. This property is approximately 1500 feet from the nearest
boundary oI the Solag site, but again, is separated by geographical
boundaries in a neighborhood with no connecting roadways to the Solag
area.
DISCUSSION
Solag Disposal Company has an exclusive lranchise with the City for all trash
collection services. The transfer site is the only location within the City limits
where Solag conducts its activities. The access road lor hauting operations is Paseo
Adelanto. There is an acknowledged significant environmental impact on the
neighborhood from this activity.
Los Rios Street borders the downtown business district. It contains a mixture of
business, residences and combined business/residences. A plan has been under
consideration for many years to relocirte Solag Disposal Company to another part ot
the City. The plan would combine the present transfer station site with adiacent
land to form a City park.
The contract on the atenda would constitute a significant component of the Solag
operation. It is possible that the franchise could not continue without it. It is a long
term agreement with the city.
The properties in question are both combined business/residences. The Jones
residence is within 100 feet of the Solag property. (Regulation 18702.3(aXl)) The
Jones business is tourist and activity oriented, and unrelated to solid waste hauling
except for the impacts on the property.
The Vasquez property is more than 300 feet and less than 2500 feet from the Solag
property. The residence is also a photography studio, a business unrelated to so.lid
waste haulin8 except lor the impacts on the property.
No appraisal has been conducted of the impact of the Solag operation upon either
property. The City Council is prepared to do so if it will result in a conclusive
decision of the issue.
There has been a series of conllict alletations in recent years in this community and
a number of similar issues lie iust ahead. San Juan Capistrano is geographically a
small community and it is difficult for anyone residing within the city limits to avoid
being within 2500 feet of any proiect of significant size. Naturally all elected
officials and all appointed commissioners must reside within the city limits.
Unfortunately, the population of 26,18).just exceeds the small jurisdiction threshold
of 251000 in Regulation 18703. t. It is hoped that decisive lormal direction from the
FPPC on early key issues such as this one will provide the necessary guidance to


Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Five
avoid repeated conflict situations and the inevitable stream of FPPC inquiries that
flow therefrom.
If we can provide any further information, please do not hesitate to call this office
immediately.
Very truly yours,
ilrkil-/JL
RICHARD K. DENHALTER
City Attorney
RKD/ef
Enclosures
